name: Trail run surrounding Puig Campana
name_original: Trail run rodeando el Puig Campana
region_intro: Jogging around Finestrat offers a diverse landscape for runners, combining challenging mountain trails with scenic coastal paths. The region is characterized by the imposing Puig Campana mountain, which provides demanding ascents and technical descents. Alongside these mountainous routes, Finestrat features flatter coastal options and trails through hilly natural parks, catering to various fitness levels. Best jogging routes around Finestrat The most popular jogging route is Trail run surrounding Puig Campana, a 9.9 miles (16.0 km) trail that takes 2 hours 48 minutes to complete. This difficult route features significant elevation gain and panoramic views around the iconic mountain. Another top favourite among local runners is View of Benidorm's skyline – Benidorm Marina loop from Castellum Romano, a moderate 4.5 miles (7.3 km) path. This route offers views of Benidorm's skyline and marina. Local runners also love the Plaza de la Cruz – Balcón del Mediterráneo loop from la Cala de Finestrat, a 8.9 miles (14.3 km) trail leading through the coastal area, often completed in about 1 hour 30 minutes. Jogging around Finestrat is defined by challenging mountain trails, scenic coastal paths, and hilly natural parks. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy jogging routes to more demanding trail running. The routes in Finestrat are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from more than 20 reviews. More than 2000 runners have used komoot to explore Finestrat's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available in Finestrat?
A: Finestrat offers a diverse selection of over 35 running routes, catering to various fitness levels. You'll find everything from easy coastal jogs to challenging mountain trails.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on running trails around Finestrat?
A: The region boasts a diverse landscape for runners. You can expect a mix of challenging mountain trails, scenic coastal paths, and routes through natural parks. This includes steep ascents and technical descents around mountains like Puig Campana, as well as flatter, often paved surfaces closer to the coast.

Q: Are there options for beginners or less experienced runners in Finestrat?
A: Yes, Finestrat has options for all levels. While many routes are challenging, you can find easier, flatter paths, especially around the coastal area of Cala Finestrat. For example, the Benidorm seafront promenade offers a flat and accessible jogging experience. There is at least one easy route listed in the area, and 13 moderate routes that might suit those looking for a less strenuous run.

Q: What about challenging routes for experienced trail runners?
A: Finestrat is an excellent destination for experienced trail runners, with 22 difficult routes available. The imposing Puig Campana mountain is a major draw, offering demanding ascents and technical descents with panoramic views. A prime example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e872627420/trail-run-surrounding-puig-campana" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Trail run surrounding Puig Campana&lt;/a&gt;, a 16 km route with significant elevation gain.

Q: Are there any circular running routes in Finestrat?
A: Yes, many of the running routes in Finestrat are circular, allowing you to start and finish at the same point. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38255782" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Plaza de la Cruz – Balcón del Mediterráneo loop from la Cala de Finestrat&lt;/a&gt; is a popular moderate circular trail that takes you through the coastal area.

Q: What are some scenic viewpoints or attractions I can see along the running routes?
A: Finestrat's routes offer stunning views and interesting landmarks. You can enjoy panoramic views from mountain trails, or coastal vistas along routes near Cala Finestrat. Notable attractions in the wider area include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/284114" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Albir Lighthouse&lt;/a&gt;, the serene &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/73676" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Guadalest Reservoir&lt;/a&gt;, and the dramatic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4631915" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Relleu Footbridge Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt;. Some routes, like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38251299" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Benidorm's skyline – Benidorm Marina loop from Castellum Romano&lt;/a&gt;, offer fantastic cityscapes.

Q: Are there any running routes that are suitable for families?
A: For families, especially those with younger children or looking for a more relaxed pace, the flatter coastal paths around Cala Finestrat or the Benidorm seafront promenade are ideal. These routes are generally less strenuous and offer pleasant scenery without significant elevation changes.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the running trails in Finestrat?
A: Many natural trails in Finestrat are suitable for running with dogs, but it's always recommended to check local regulations for specific areas, especially within natural parks like Sierra de Finestrat. Ensure your dog is on a leash and that you carry water for both of you, particularly during warmer months.

Q: What do other runners enjoy most about jogging in Finestrat?
A: The running routes in Finestrat are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 20 reviews. Runners frequently praise the diverse terrain, from challenging mountain trails to scenic coastal paths, and the stunning panoramic views that can be enjoyed along many of the routes.

Q: Are there any running routes near Benidorm?
A: Yes, Finestrat is very close to Benidorm, and several routes offer views of or extend into Benidorm. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40382666" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Benidorm's skyline – Benidorm Marina loop from Cala Finestrat&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 14.2 km route that provides excellent views of the city's skyline and marina. The Benidorm seafront promenade itself is also a popular, flat option for running.

Q: What is the best time of year to go running in Finestrat?
A: Finestrat offers good running conditions for much of the year. Spring and autumn provide pleasant temperatures, ideal for both coastal and mountain runs. During the summer, it's best to run early in the morning or late in the evening to avoid the midday heat. Winters are generally mild, making it possible to run comfortably throughout the day.

Q: Are there any routes that offer a mix of coastal and mountain scenery?
A: Yes, Finestrat's unique location between the sea and mountains allows for routes that combine both. While specific routes might lean more towards one or the other, you can often find trails that start near the coast and ascend into the lower hills, offering a blend of sea views and rugged landscapes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/33815292" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tower of Les Caletes – Balcón del Mediterráneo loop from Cala Finestrat&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult route that starts near the coast and offers varied terrain.
